| tollfree= | fax=+358 9 150-901 | price= | checkin= | checkout= | content= }} ==Stay safe== The Kallio area is generally a bit rougher than the rest of the city and is as close as Helsinki gets to a red light district. However, it is a lot more tame than most 'such parts of town' in Europe. You will be fine there at any hour as long as you look out not to get in between a fight of two drunkards about who the remaining vodka belongs to. ==Connect== ==Go next== * [[Helsinki/Central|Helsinki's central]] * [[Helsinki/Eastern suburbs|Helsinki's eastern suburbs]] * [[Linnanmäki]] {{IsPartOf|Helsinki}} {{usabledistrict}} {{geo|60.1862|24.9535}} {{mapmask|60.1796, 24.9389|60.1764, 24.9505|60.1770, 24.9658|60.1741, 24.9766|60.197, 24.996|60.217, 24.982|60.221, 24.946|60.20, 24.932}}

{{pagebanner|pgname=Kamppi and Southwestern Helsinki|WV banner Helsinki Southwest Hietalahti waterfront.jpg}}
'''Kamppi and Southwestern [[Helsinki]]''' is here defined as the part of the city raying out to the south-west of the [[Helsinki/Central|city core]], stretching along highway 51 to the border of next-door [[Espoo]]. Kamppi is for the purposes of this article delimited by Baana and Hietaniemi cemetery in the north-west, [[Mannerheimintie]] in the east and Bulevardi in the south-east. In addition to Kamppi, Ruoholahti and Jätkäsaari the article also covers Lauttasaari.
{{mapframe|zoom=13|60.161|24.910}}
South-western Helsinki has something for everyone. The part next to Mannerheimintie with the shopping centres of Kamppi and Forum are not much different from the rest of the centre, though just a block or two into Kamppi the environment becomes much more residential. There is no lack of restaurants, cafés, bars and clubs, though and aside of the centre itself and [[Helsinki/Inner East|Kallio]], this is a good place to go out for a drink.
Ruoholahti, still in the 1980s a somewhat rough and dirty part of the city is now modern and clean with glass and steel office and apartment buildings, partially built on land reclaimed from the sea. Still the former cable factory (nowadays a cultural centre), the Salmisaari power plant and the shipyard reminds of the former industrial character. A bridge to the west lays the island of Lauttasaari, mostly residential.
The island of Lauttasaari is the second-biggest island in Finland by population, after the island of Fasta Åland in the Åland archipelago. The name "Lauttasaari" literally means "ferry island" as in the past the only way to reach this island was by ferry, but nowadays there is a bridge.
==Get in== The eastern parts of this district are practically part of the centre and the rest of Kamppi within walking distance from it. Ruoholahti is best accessible by metro or tram 9 from the central and eastern parts of the city, by tram 8 from the [[Helsinki/West|Töölö]]. The new metro extension takes you all the way to Lauttasaari and beyond.
If you travel in from [[Espoo]] or western [[Uusimaa]] by bus or metro, this part of Helsinki is the first you will reach. The same is true if you're arriving from [[Tallinn]] on Tallink's or Eckerö Line's ferries, from [[St. Petersburg]] by ferry or on a [[Cruising the Baltic Sea|massive cruise ship]] that can't dock in central Helsinki.
To the ferry terminals, take tram 7.
==Get around== Getting around in the western parts is no struggle, you can use the buses, the trams or the metro to get wherever you want, not forgetting going afoot. Even though the options for public transport are great, don't immerse yourself only in them and remember the option of going on foot; it might save your time since especially the trams might be slow.
==See== [[File:Vanha kirkko ruttopuisto.jpg|thumbnail|Vanha Kirkko — The Old Church]] [[File:Alexander Theatre Helsinki 2011-09-03 1.jpg|thumbnail|The Alexander theatre]]
* {{see | name=Helsinki Old Church | alt=Helsingin Vanha Kirkko | url= | email= | address= | lat=60.1663 | long=24.9396 | directions=between Lönnrotinkatu, Bulevardi, Georginkatu and Annankatu | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A neoclassical wooden church built in the early 19th century, which is the oldest church in central Helsinki. It's in the middle of the Park of the Old Church, which is also known as the Plague Park (Ruttopuisto) because it was used as a cemetery for many of the victims of the plague of 1710. }} * {{see | name=Lönnrotinpuistikko | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=60.16677 | long=24.93881 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2015-09-16 | content=A tiny little park next to the Old church. In the middle there's a statue of 19th century author and scientist Elias Lönnrot (author of ''Kalevala'', the Finnish national epic). }} * {{see | name=Amos Andersson Art Gallery | alt= | url=http://www.amosanderson.fi/#page=t1&lang=en | email= | address=Yrjönkatu 23 | lat=60.16834 | long=24.93740 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M Th F 10:00–18:00, W 10:00–20:00, Sa Su 11:00–17:00 | price=Adults €10 | content=In the Forum block. The largest private gallery of contemporary art in Finland. }} * {{see | name=Helsinki synagogue | alt= | url=http://www.jchelsinki.fi/en | email= | address=Malminkatu 26 | lat=60.16737 | long=24.92839| directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikidata=Q2614280 |wikipedia=Helsinki Synagogue | lastedit=2015-09-16 | content=The centre of the [[Judaism|Jewish community]] in Helsinki; there are also a Jewish school, kindergarten and a kosher store on the premises. You probably have to call ahead for visiting. }} * {{see | name=Metropolia Polytech main building | alt= | url= | email= | address= |lat=60.16312 | long=24.93119 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2015-09-16 | content=This impressive 19th-century building next to the Hietalahti square hosted the Helsinki Technical University until the 1950s, nowadays it's the main building for the Metropolia University of Applied Sciences. As you're there, check out the Hietalahti market hall and market square. }} * {{see | name=Aleksanterin teatteri | alt= | url=http://www.aleksanterinteatteri.fi/page-en/ | email= | address=Albertinkatu 32 | lat=60.16355 | long=24.93351 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2015-09-16 | content=The Alexander theater, named after the Russian czar Alexander II and finished in 1879 is likely Helsinki's most beautiful venue for performance art. Occasional shows and concerts in English. }} * {{see | name=Lasipalatsi | alt= | url=https://glaspalatset.fi | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia= | wikidata=Q630304 | lastedit=2019-11-11 | content=Another of Helsinki's most iconic [[Functionalist architecture in Finland|functionalist]] buildings right next to Mannerheimintie, the "glass palace" by Viljo Revell, Heimo Riihimäki and Niilo Kokko was finished in 1936 and is functionalist both on the inside and outside. It was planned as a temporary office building and shopping mall, to be replaced by a bigger one in the future but that never happened. In the 1980s and 90s the building was in a fairly sorry state but due to opposition by citizens and the Board of Antiquities it wasn't demolished but underwent a major restoration at the turn of the millennium. Today the gorgeous white building with colourful [[signs|neon signs]] houses shops, restaurants, the movie theatre Bio Rex, and beneath it, the art museum Amos Rex. }} * {{see | name=Amos Rex | alt= | url=https://amosrex.fi/en/ | email= | address=Mannerheimintie 22–24 | lat=60.170650 | long=24.936405 | directions=entrance located at the right hand side of Lasipalatsi, in the near vicinity of the central station | phone=+358 9 6844-4633 (bookings), +358 9 684-4460 (customer service) | tollfree= | hours=M 11–20; W–F 11–20; Sa/Su 11–17, closed on Tu | price=General admission €20, reduced €15, students and youth under 30 years €5, children under 18 years free | wikidata=Q22662489 | lastedit=2023-11-28 | content=A modern arts museum that tends towards immersive, often audiovisual installations and has rapidly gained in acknowledgement since its opening in 2018. While entrance and shop are in Lasipalatsi, the actual museum is a piece architecture playing with organic forms located below the square located behind the visible building; sculptural forms extend into the square, portholes on their top allow a glimpse into the museum. }} * {{see | name=Crusell Bridge | alt=Crusellinsilta | url= | email= | address=Länsisatamankatu | lat=60.1603 | long=24.9082 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia= Crusell Bridge | wikidata= Q10462047 | lastedit=2023-09-26 | content=A cable-stayed bridge, which connects the districts of Ruoholahti and Jätkäsaari. It is named after the Swedish-Finnish composer and clarinetist Bernhard Henrik Crusell (1775–1838). }} [[File:Platform area at Koivusaari metro station empty of people on an evening in May 2025.jpg|thumb|right|Inside Koivusaari metro station, the only underwater metro station in the world]] * {{see | name=Koivusaari metro station | alt= | url= | email= | address=Sotkatie | lat=60.163175 | long=24.855957 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2024-11-10 | content=The only underwater metro station in the world, although in practice you won't notice it as the sea around the station isn't visible from inside the station. Also includes the longest escalators in Finland. Metro trains run in both directions every 10 minutes or so. }}
In addition the district of Ruoholahti is one of the few places in Helsinki to see modern glass and steel architecture, and of course the Crusell bridge across Ruoholahti bay. Lauttasaari is one of the greenest areas in Helsinki. The island's landmark, the 1950s water tower, was dismantled in 2015.
==Do== [[File:La câblerie Kaapeli (Helsinki) (2768446714).jpg|thumbnail|Kaapeli]] [[File:Uimahalli - N30836 - hkm.HKMS000005-km003k5j.jpg|thumb|Yrjönkatu swimming hall (in 1929)]] [[File:Uusi Sauna in November.jpg|thumb|right|The entrance to Uusi Sauna]]
* {{do | name=Aleksanterin teatteri | alt= | url=http://www.aleksanterinteatteri.fi/page-en/ | email= | address=Bulevardi 23-27 | lat=60.16342 | long=24.93358 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= }} * {{do | name=Annantalo | alt= | url=http://www.annantalo.fi/en/home | email= | address=Annankatu 30 | lat=60.16778 | long=24.93463 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 08:00–20:00, Sa Su 10:00–16:00 | price= | content=Feel creative? Annantalo, a multimodal "house of art" arranges art workshops and courses for both kids and grownups, as well as performances and exhibitions. }} * {{do | name=The Cable Factory | alt=Kaapelitehdas or just Kaapeli | url=http://www.kaapelitehdas.fi/en | email= | address=Tallberginkatu 1 | lat=60.1620 | long=24.9037 | directions=tram 8 | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=The former cable factory in Ruoholahti is nowadays a multi-purpose culture centre. It hosts concerts, fairs, art workshops and odd/original museums like the restaurant museum and photography museum. There is a Restaurant/Café/Bar [http://www.himasali.com/english/ Hima & Sali] in the building. At lunch time you can choose your meal from a daily menu or watch the cook prepare your wok from ingredients you selected. Good place for vegetarians. }} * {{do | name=Huone | alt= | url=http://www.huone.fi/ | email= | address=Länsisatamankatu 16 | lat=60.15939 | long=24.90971 | directions= | phone=+358 20-785-1440 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=08:00–24:00 mostly | price=€44-59 per person | lastedit=2016-04-06 | content=Huone ("Room") claims to be the world's first event hotel, providing premises and services for their customers to hold their own events. There are twelve rooms of different sizes, and each can be rented for a day, a morning or an evening. Huone can host various kinds of events such as business meetings, birthday parties or sauna evenings. The customer holds the event; Huone provides the premises and services, including catering, with alcoholic drinks or without. }} * {{do | name=Kasinonranta Beach, Lauttasaari | alt= | url= | email= | address=Tiirasaarentie| lat=60.155059| long=24.871839 | directions=Buses 20N, 55A and 66A, stop Hevosenkenkäpuisto. | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= A popular place in summer with a large playground for children and a pleasant café [http://www.kasinonranta.fi/ Kasinonranta] (in summer season open M-F 15:00-19:00, Sa Su 10:00-19:00). At the beach and the park around you can picnic, kick a ball, play a beach volleyball, swim, canoe or skatefurf. In winter it is possible to snowkite here or hike, skate or cross country ski on frozen sea around Lauttasaari island and even reach some nearby islands. }} * {{do | name=Outdoor skating Lauttasaari | alt= | url=https://www.hel.fi/helsinki/en/culture/sports/outdoor/artificial-and-skating-rinks/lauttasaari-sports-park-skating-rink {{dead link|December 2020}} | email= | address=Lahnalahdentie 2 | lat=60.163606 | long=24.867423 | directions=Short walk from Lahnalahdentie bus stop (lines 102, 110, 147, 154, 167) or Lahnalahden puisto bus stop (lines 20, 65A, 66A) | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 08:00-21:00 | price= | content=Two outdoor skating rings, open approximately 2 Jan - 24 Feb, depending on weather. }} * {{do | name=Little Theater | alt=Lilla Teatern | url=http://www.hkt.fi/lillateatern/ | email= | address=Eerikinkatu 2 | lat=60.16785 | long=24.93746 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= }} * {{do | name=Salmisaaren Liikuntakeskus | url=http://www.salmisaarenliikuntakeskus.fi/ | email= | address=Energiakatu 3 | lat=60.1659 | long=24.9037 | directions=next to Helsinki Energia @ Ruoholahti | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=New sports mall in Helsinki, includes indoor beach, bowling, ice hockey, wall climbing, gym, Megazone, restaurant and lots more... }} * {{do | name=Tennispalatsi | alt= | url=http://www.finnkino.fi/cinemas/helsinki_tennispalatsi/ | email= | address=Salomonkatu 15 | lat=60.16946 | long=24.93071 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2015-12-13 | content=The Tennispalatsi ("Tennis palace") was built to house tennis events for the 1940 and 1952 Olympics. Nowadays, it retains no trace of its Olympic past. Instead, it functions primarily as Helsinki's biggest movie theatre, with more than ten halls on two floors, showing movies both from Finland and abroad. As well as functioning as a movie theatre, Tennispalatsi also houses cultural exhibitions, small stores, restaurants (including Pizza Hut and Hesburger) and cafés. }} * {{do | name=Uusi Sauna | alt= | url=https://www.uusisauna.fi/ | email= | address=Välimerenkatu 10 | lat=60.159246 | long=24.9182 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Tu-Sa 16:00-00:00, closed Su M | price=Sauna €18, beer around €10 | lastedit=2023-11-30 | content=A stylish and spacious public sauna and bar in Jätkäsaari. Separate saunas for men and women. You can bring your own towel or rent one from the bar. The bar has a diverse selection of drinks and you can bring your drink to the dressing room (not to the actual sauna though). The bar has live music playing on special event nights. }} * {{do | name=Yrjönkatu swimming hall | alt=Yrjönkadun uimahalli | url=https://liikunta.hel.fi/en/venues/tprek:41102?returnPath=%2Fhaku%3Fq%3Duimahalli%26searchType%3DVenue%26venueId%3Dtprek%253A41102 | email= | address=Yrjönkatu 21b | lat=60.168114 | long=24.938966 | directions=across from the Torni Hotel | phone=+358 9 310-87401 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikidata=Q2601487 | content=An art-deco bath house with historic architecture. Three types of saunas and a swimming pool. Take a sauna and swim in the nude. There are separate days for women and men. Bathing suits are not banned, but almost everyone goes without one. Men's swimming days are: Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday; Women's days: Monday, Wednesday, Friday, Sunday. 1st Floor €5 (or €5.40 with a lockable stall or 'cabin'), 2nd Floor €14. The second floor (accessible only the afternoons from Tuesday to Sunday) includes access to a steam sauna and a wood-heated sauna, as well as a café. }}
==Buy== [[File:2022-06 Glaspalatset Lasipalatsi Kulturkasernen Åbo kasern Mannerheimvägen 01.jpg|thumbnail|300px|View of Kamppi with Lasipalatsi and Kamppi centre]]
* {{buy | name=Kamppi Centre | alt=Kampin Keskus | url=http://www.kamppi.fi/en/ | email= | address= | lat=60.16923 | long= 24.93286 | directions=Trams: 2, 9 Metro: Kamppi. | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Big shopping mall. Plenty of international brands and restaurants. Long-distance bus terminal in the basement. }} * {{buy | name=Forum | alt= | url=http://www.forum.fi | email= | address= | lat=60.16933 | long=24.93810 | directions=corner of Simonkatu and Mannerheimintie | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2015-09-16 | content=Kamppi centre's next-door older brother with plenty of shops, restaurants and other services. }} * {{buy | name=Kauppakeskus Ruoholahti | alt=| url= http://www.kauppakeskusruoholahti.fi/| email= | address= | lat=60.1637 | long=24.9107 | directions=Metro: Ruoholahti. Tram: 8 | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Quite small compared to the suburban malls along the ring roads, but this urban mall still has a Citymarket and a couple of speciality stores and cafes. }} * {{buy | name=Verkkokauppa.com | alt= | url=http://www.verkkokauppa.com/ | email= | address= | lat=60.1564 | long=24.9209 | directions=metro: Ruoholahti, entails some walking; tram: 9; bus: 15A | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= 2 km from Helsinki centre in the Western Harbour (Länsisatama). Northern Europe's and possibly Europe's largest home-electronics store with 20,000 m² of retail space. The best spot for electronics, computers, digital cameras, mobile phones and gadgets in general in Helsinki. They not only sell electronics but also imported snacks (mainly from the USA) to enjoy while watching TV or barbecuing - think soda, chips, barbecue sauce, candies and such. In addition to being a store it features a couple of fast food outlets one of them with Australian meat pies, and a computer game museum on the 5th floor. Literally on the top of it all, the 7th floor features Finland's largest sightseeing terrace with views of Helsinki, the sea and prime view of cruise ships in the summer and in the middle of it a real MiG-21BIS fighter plane, the entrance is free. Free parking underground and on an outdoor parking area behind the complex. }} * {{buy | name=Ruslania | alt=Ruslania Books Oy | url=http://www.ruslania.com | email=customerservice@ruslania.com | address=Bulevardi 7 | lat=60.1649 | long=24.9383 | directions=buses 14, 18 and 21 stop near the store; the trams 1, 3 and 6 stop right next to it and tram is the best way to get there from the north | phone=+358 50-388-9439 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=09:00-18:00, 09:00-16:00 | price= | image=Ruslania - Bulevardi 7 - Kamppi - Helsinki - m.jpg | lastedit=2018-03-09 | content=Ruslania offers Finnish and Russian products (which includes for example souvenirs, literature, music, candy, posters and cards). A nice, not that big shop full of mostly Russian products which makes it a heaven for lovers of Russian culture or Soviet-y stuff. }}
===Shops=== * {{buy | name=Helsinki 10 | alt= | url=http://www.helsinki10.fi/ {{dead link|December 2020}} | email= | address=Eerikinkatu 3 | lat= 60.16773 | long= 24.93637 | directions= | phone=+358 10-548-9801 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= M–F 11:00–20:00, Sa 11:00-18:00 | price= | content=This bright-white "lifestyle department store" sells both international and Finnish (designer) labels such as Raf Simons, Wood Wood, Acne and April77 as well as second-hand clothes, accessories, records, magazines etc. }} * {{buy | name=Helsingin Pantti| alt= | url=https://pantti.fi/en/toimipiste/helsinki-kamppi/ | email=kamppi@helsinginpantti.fi | address=Salomonkatu 3 | lat= 60.1705 | long=24.9342 | directions=in Kamppi | phone=+358 9 2705-3030 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M–F 10:00–17:00 | price= | image=Helsingin Pantti Salomonkatu 3 Helsinki 20231117.jpg | content=A pawn shop. }} * {{buy | name=Paloni | alt= | url=http://www.paloni.fi/ | email= | address=Eerikinkatu 7 | lat= 60.16727 | long= 24.93515 | directions= | phone=+358 50-589-4131 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Open M-F 11:00-19:00, Sa 11:00-16:00 | price= | content=Paloni is a creative design concept store. It sells items that are designed and made by over 70 independent designers. Paloni's product range covers women's clothing, accessories, jewellery, home decor, gift items and children's clothing. }} * {{buy | name=Keltainen Jäänsärkijä | alt= | url=http://www.kolumbus.fi/levykauppa/ | email= | address=Urho Kekkosenkatu 4-6 A | lat=60.16839 | long=24.93269 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Helsinki's largest indie store, covers virtually everything except classical and electronic. Lots of indie vinyl at reasonable prices. }} * {{buy | name=Keltainen Ruusu| alt= | url=https://keltainenruusu.fi/ | email=myynti@keltainenruusu.fi | address=Malminrinne 2–4 | lat= 60.167038 | long=24.930693 | directions=in Kamppi; about 100 meters from the metro station | phone=+358 9 6855-5270 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su–Th 12:00–03:00, F–Sa 12:00–06:00 | price= | content=A sex store. }} * {{buy | name=Compact Records (Dark Side of the)| alt= | url= | email= | address=Lönnrotinkatu 23 | lat= 60.16484 | long=24.93376 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= }} * {{buy | name=Siisti Kirppis | alt= | url=https://siistikirppis.fi/ | email=info@siistikirppis.fi | address=Suezinkatu 3 | lat= 60.15845 | long=24.92043 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu–F 11:00–19:00, Weekend 12:00–17:00 | price= | content=A flea market. }}
===Markets=== [[File:2009-09-19-helsinki-by-RalfR-018.jpg|thumbnail|Flea market on Hietalahti square, to the left the market hall, straight ahead the old technical university]]
* {{buy | name=Hietalahti Old Market Hall | alt= | url=http://www.hietalahdenkauppahalli.fi | email= | address=Hietalahdentori | lat= 60.16303 | long= 24.92947 | directions=tram 6 | phone=+358 9 670-145 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=One of Helsinki's three traditional market halls until the turn of the millennium, after that concentrating on antiques and as of 2019 a kind of a food court, with some gift shops. }} * {{buy | name=Hietalahti Open Air Flea market | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=60.16259 | long= 24.93003 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Next to Hietalahti Old Market Hall, this is the most popular flea market in Helsinki. Open year round, but busiest from May to August. }}
==Eat== ===Budget=== * {{eat | name=Barbarossa | alt= | url= | email= | address=Yrjönkatu 29 | lat=60.16856 | long= 24.93714 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Good price-quality ratio pizzaplace, always fast service. Very small location though, so you might have to eat your pizza elsewhere. }} * {{eat | name=Eerikin Pippuri | alt= | url=http://www.eerikinpippuri.com | email= | address=Eerikinkatu 17 | lat= 60.16656 | long=24.93311 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=One of the more popular kebab places in town. On the same street as many bars, so good for a midnight meal as well. }} * {{eat | name=Pompier | alt=VPK | url= | email= | address=Albertinkatu 29 | lat=60.16491 | long=24.93277 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 11:00-15:00 | price=€12.60 per head. | lastedit=2015-05-20 | content=Run by the Volunteer Fire Brigade, this restaurant serves a daily changing buffet of hearty Finnish fare in a cafeteria straight from the 1950s, complete with grim portraits of moustached Hosemasters staring down at you. Pea soup and pancakes on Thursday are particularly popular. }} * {{eat | name=Santa Clara | alt= | url=https://santaclara.fi/ | email=santaclararestaurantbar@gmail.com | address=Kuubankatu 3 | lat=60.1545 | long=24.9085 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 44-989-9969 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=A lunch restaurant with bar. }} * {{eat | name=Shinobi | alt= | url=https://www.shinobi.fi/ | email=shinobi@shinobi.fi | address=Albertinkatu 38 | lat=60.16499 | long=24.93211 | directions= | phone=+358 10-229-3062 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-Th 17:00-23:00, F-Sa 16:00-00:00 | price= | lastedit=2023-04-27 | content=A Japanese restaurant featuring classic and contemporary dishes. }} * {{eat | name=Singapore Hot Wok | alt= | url=http://www.kamppi.fi/RetailerHome.aspx?Unit_no=029 | email= | address=Kamppi Shopping Centre, E floor, Urho Kekkosen katu 5 B | lat= 60.16849 | long=24.93176 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=€9.50 per head. | content=Select from a few tasty plates of Wok. }} * {{eat | name=Sky Express | alt= | url=http://skyexpress.fi/ | email= | address=Annankatu 31 | lat=60.16811 | long=24.93547 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A pizza spot very close to the city center. It's a relatively small place, but the service is very fast and the place is open late at night. Opens around 11:00, and closes at 23:00 (22:00 on Sundays). Try the ''Päivän jättipizza'' ("Daily giant pizza"), which is a large, thin pizza with a varying selection of fillings plus a 0.4 liter soft drink for €6, which is almost unbeatably cheap. They also offer a liter of beer + the daily giant pizza for €13. }} * {{eat | name=Yeti-Nepal | alt= | url=http://www.yetinepal.fi/ | email=yetinep@gmail.com | address=Itämerenkatu 12 | lat=60.163285| long=24.915527 | directions= | phone=+358 9 693-3010 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:30-22:00, F 10:30-23:00, Sa 12:00-23:00, Su 12:00-22:00 | price=Main dishes €10-15, lunch menu from {{EUR|8.50}} | content=One of many Nepalese places. It might not be the cheapest one, but the food is great. The restaurant is well visited and often full at lunch time. }} * {{eat | name=Osakuntabaari | alt= | url=https://hys.net/osakuntabaari/ | email=osakuntabaari@hys.net | address=Urho Kekkosen katu 4-6 D | lat=60.168339 | long=24.93264 | directions=The door to the right of Tavastia's main door, press the door buzzer | phone=+358 50-325-2698 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 11:00-15:00 | price=€8.50, dessert €1.30 | lastedit=2022-06-08 | content=A student restaurant that offers affordable and delicious lunches also for outsiders. }} * {{eat | name=Pappa Peruna | alt= | url=https://www.pappaperuna.fi/ | email=info@pappaperuna.fi | address=Välimerenkatu 5 D | lat=60.1598 | long=24.9179 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 44-244-2787 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= }} * {{eat | name=Sodexo Restaurant Sisäpiha | alt= | url=https://www.sodexo.fi/en/restaurants/restaurant-sisapiha | email=ravintola.sisapiha@sodexo.com | address=Lapinlahdenkatu 1 B | lat=60.166405 | long=24.931985 | directions= | phone=+358 50-596-9116 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 11:00-13:30 | price=€10.90 | lastedit=2022-06-08 | content=Lunch restaurant. }}
===Mid-range=== [[File:Eerikinkatu 14 Helsinki.jpg|thumbnail|Eerikinkatu 14. Typical view in eastern Kamppi]] [[File:Adana köfte at restaurant Halikarnas (bright).jpg|thumb|right|''Adana köfte'' (Turkish lamb meatballs)]] [[File:Interior of restaurant Meritähti.jpg|thumb|right|Restaurant interior]]
* {{eat | name=Baba's House | alt= | url=https://www.babashouse.fi/| email=info@babashouse.fi | address=Atlantinkatu 10 | lat=60.1546 | long=24.9100 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 41-715-8870 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=A pizzeria. }} * {{eat | name=Bites Burgers | alt= | url=http://www.bites.fi | email= | address=Lapinlahdenkatu 3 | lat=60.16651 | long=24.9312 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price=€10-12 | lastedit=2019-07-25 | content=Tasty burgers made to order, with a regular, vegan, vegetarian and special option that can be further customised. They have a good selection of sodas and craft beer. In their Kamppi location there's also an inner yard open during the summer with live music most nights. They also have a restaurant in [[Helsinki/Inner East|Vallila]] and in the [[Helsinki/Central|Kaivopiha food court]]. }} * {{eat | name=Boneless Jätkäsaari | alt= | url=https://www.boneless.fi/ | email= | address=Välimerenkatu 24 | lat=60.1575 | long=24.9118 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 45-342-0502 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=A hamburger restaurant. }} * {{eat | name=Empire Plaza | alt= | url=http://www.empireplaza.fi/ | email= | address=Urho Kekkosen katu 1, Kamppi Shopping Centre | lat=60.1692 | long=24.93404 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price=Main dishes €7-20, lunch buffet €8.50 | content=Tasty Chinese food. }} * {{eat | name=Faro | alt= | url=http://www.faroravintola.fi | email=myynti@faroravintola.fi | address=Kellosaarenranta 2 | lat=60.16089 | long=24.90752 | directions= | phone=+358 10-424-9830 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Lunch €10 to €13, main courses €20 to €30 | lastedit=2017-11-30 | content=A rather nice restaurant and bar located right at the seashore, offering nice views. Serves lunch and à la carte menu. Lunch is affordable, if not quite cheap, but the à la carte menu is a bit on the pricier side. Also functions as a bar. Serves a variety of cuisine, lunch varies daily. }} * {{eat | name=Fisken på Disken | alt= | url=http://www.fisken.fi | email=info@fisken.fi | address=Urho Kekkosen katu 1 | lat=60.16955 | long=24.93393 | directions= | phone=+358 40-197-1157 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=11:00-21:00 | price=Main courses €10 to €28 | lastedit=2016-12-19 | content=The name means "The fish on the table" in Swedish, and the restaurant indeed focuses almost exclusively on fish and seafood. Located on the fifth floor of the Kamppi shopping centre amongst other restaurants, easy to find as it's right next to the escalators. Rather crowded so reservations are recommended, although not mandatory. Try the fish and chips, available at €16. }} * {{eat | name=Halikarnas | alt= | url=http://www.ravintolahalikarnas.fi | email= | address=Ruoholahdenkatu 18 | lat=60.16508 | long=24.92639 | directions= | phone=+358 9 685-1862 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=11:00-23:00 mostly | price=Buffet around €10, à la carte dishes €14–22 | lastedit=2015-10-26 | content=One of the most famous Turkish restaurants in Helsinki. Famous for its Mediterranean-style buffet, served not only on working days but on weekends also (it's a bit more expensive on weekends though), but also serves quality Turkish à la carte dishes. The coffee, although served from a nondescript thermos can, is one of the best coffees available in Helsinki. Spacious with stylish Turkish decor, with artwork depicting ancient Mediterranean cities on the walls. }} * {{eat | name=India House | alt= | url=http://indiahouse.fi | email=indiahouse@indiahouse.fi | address=Simonkatu 8, Forum |lat=60.16939 | long=24.93837 | directions=Tram 2, 9 | phone=+358 50-463-0155 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 10:30-22:30, Su 12:00-23:00 | price=Mains around €20, lunch €10 | content=The only Indian restaurant in Helsinki accepted as decent by Indians here. }} * {{eat | name=Ka-Shing | alt= | url= | email= | address=Lauttasaarentie 14 | lat=60.1607 | long=24.8868 | directions= | phone=+358 9 676-319 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 10:30-21:00, Sa Su 12:00-21:00 | price= | lastedit=2015-02-26 | content=Probably the best Chinese restaurant on the residential and office district of Lauttasaari. Rather small, and can be quite packed at lunch time. Lunch is very cheap, usually €7-8, but on evenings and weekends à la carte courses cost €12 - 20. The restaurant provides Donald Duck comic books in Finnish for the customers to read. }} * {{eat | name=Konstan Möljä | alt= | url=https://www.konstanmolja.fi/ | email= | address=Hietalahdenkatu 14 | lat=60.16394 | long=24.92675 | directions= | phone=+358 9 694-7504 | tollfree= | hours= | price=Lunch buffet €13.70, dinner buffet €38 | content=Traditional Finnish food. Very nice if you want to try all kinds of Finnish meals! }} * {{eat | name=Kosmos | alt= | url=https://kosmos.fi/en/front-page/ | email= | address=Kalevankatu 3 | lat=60.16795 | long=24.93949 | directions=Trams: 3, 4, 6, 10 | phone=+358 9 647-255 | tollfree= | hours= | price=Mains €15-25 | content=A Helsinki institution dating to 1924, proudly serving "Helsinkian" food – a melange of Russian, French and Swedish influences. Try one of the three classics: Vorschmack with duchess potatoes, the Sylvester Sandwich ''au gratin'' and Baltic herrings with mashed potatoes. }} * {{eat | name=Krung Thep Thai Bistro | alt= | url=https://www.krungthepthaibistro.fi/en | email= | address=Bermudankatu 5 | lat=60.1548 | long=24.9076 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 50-351-1336 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=A Thai restaurant. }} * {{eat | name=Limone | alt= | url=https://lauttasaari.limone.fi/ | email=lauttasaari@limone.fi | address=Nahkahousuntie 1 | lat=60.15106 | long=24.88549 | directions= | phone=+358 50-562-5010 | tollfree= | hours=11:00-21:00 mostly | price=Main courses €13-26 | lastedit=2017-03-06 | content=A real Italian restaurant in the mostly residential district of Lauttasaari, serving authentic Italian cuisine, mostly pizzas and pastas. Easy to arrive at as it's located almost right next to the terminus of bus line 21V. Open for lunch (weekdays only) and à la carte dining. }} * {{eat | name=Mai Thai | alt= | url= | email= | address=Annankatu 31-33 | lat=60.16813 | long= 24.93549 | directions= | phone=+358 9 685-6850 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=One of the best Thai restaurants in Helsinki. Make sure to reserve a table in advance, and heed the chilli ratings when ordering. }} * {{eat | name=Meritähti | alt= | url=http://www.meritahti.net/ | email=ravintola@meritahti.net | address=Itämerenkatu 14 | lat=60.16266 | long=24.91432 | directions= | phone=+358 451712277 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=11:00-23:00 mostly | price= | lastedit=2015-03-28 | content=The name means "starfish", but despite that, there is actually very little seafood on the menu. The menu is very meat-oriented, with the restaurant specialising in Hungarian cuisine. The restaurant is rather small, and about half of it consists of a pub area. Very easily accessible as it is next door to the Ruoholahti metro station. Main courses €12-30. }}<!-- -->[[File:Restaurant Morton at night.jpg|thumb|right|Restaurant Morton is built from old cargo containers.]] * {{eat | name=Morton | alt= | url=https://morton.fi/konttiravintola/helsinki/ | email=helsinki@morton.fi | address=Ruoholahdenranta 8 | lat=60.16287 | long=24.92248 | directions= | phone=+358 400-805-062 | tollfree= | hours=10:00-21:00 | price=Burgers around €16 | lastedit=2021-08-27 | content=A restaurant specialising in tasty burgers. Various burgers available with either beef, mutton, chicken or vegetarian options. The entire restaurant has been built from old cargo containers, giving it a unique appearance. }} * {{eat | name=Mt. Everest | alt= | url=http://www.everestyeti.fi/ | email= | address=Lapinlahdenkatu 17 | lat= 60.16706 | long=24.92760 | directions= | phone=+358 9 6831-5450 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Main dishes €10-20 | content=Good Nepalese food. }} * {{eat | name=Pikku-Nepal | alt= | url= | email= | address=Annankatu 29 | lat=60.16791 | long=24.93581 | directions= | phone=+358 9 693-1778 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Main dishes €13–25 | content=A very good Nepalese restaurant. Good vegetarian options. }} * {{eat | name=Ravintola Poiju | alt= | url=https://www.ravintolapoiju.com/en/home | email=contact@ravintolapoiju.com | address=Jaalaranta 5 | lat=60.1614 | long=24.91459 | directions=in Jätkäsaari; next to the Ruoholahti canal | phone=+358 9 685-6638 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=An Indian-European restaurant. }} * {{eat | name=Restaurant Lasipalatsi | alt=Ravintola Lasipalatsi | url=https://www.ravintolalasipalatsi.fi/ravintola-helsinki/?lang=en | email=ravintola@ravintolalasipalatsi.fi | address=Mannerheimintie 22-24 | lat=60.17037 | long=24.93685 | directions=at Lasipalatsi | phone=+358 20-742-4290 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Main dishes €24–36 | content=A unique restaurant complex with pleasant café in the heart of the capital. }} * {{eat | name=Salve| alt= | url=http://ravintolasalve.fi/ | email= | address=Hietalahdenranta 11 | lat= 60.16215 | long=24.92874 | directions= | phone=+358 10-7664-280 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Harbour restaurant. Salve's operations began in 1897 as a small food kiosk for sailors. Even now, you can catch the feeling of Salve's past – a time when ships were made of wood and men were made of steel! Herrings of Salve you should taste. }} * {{eat | name=Suburritos | alt= | url=http://suburritos.fi/?lang=en | email=suburritos@suburritos.fi | address=Yrjönkatu 29 | lat=60.168367 | long=24.9374879 | directions= | phone=+358 441-709-0777 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=11:00-21:00 mostly | price=Buffet and burritos €9, main courses €13-25 | lastedit=2015-12-17 | content=Mexican-style restaurant near the Forum shopping centre. Famous for its lunch buffet, where you can fill up tortillas and tacos with toppings of your own choosing. }} * {{eat | name=Urbine | alt= | url= | email= | address=Tyynenmerenkatu 7 | lat=60.1571 | long=24.9214 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 45-692-4310 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=A living room style cafe-restaurant. }} * {{eat | name=Uuno Pizza | alt= | url=https://www.uunopizza.fi/ | email=info@uunopizza.fi | address=Atlantinkatu 20 B | lat=60.1523 | long=24.9111 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 400-406-199 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content= }} * {{eat | name=Sen Việt Vegan Restaurant | alt= | url=https://senvietsuomi.com/en/trang-chu | email=senviet.suomi@gmail.com | address=Atlantinkatu 18 | lat=60.1532 | long=24.9104 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 41-311-3868 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=A Vietnamese vegetarian restaurant. }}
===Splurge=== * {{eat | name=Kabuki | alt= | url=http://www.kabuki.fi/ | email= | address=Lapinlahdenkatu 12 | lat=60.1672 | long=24.9242 | directions= | phone=+358 9 694-9446 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Helsinki's best-known Japanese restaurant and a favorite of Finnish celebrities, which explains the signed ice hockey jerseys and Star Wars memorabilia scattered throughout. Alas, while the food is decent, it's not quite the real thing. Reservations recommended. Closed Sat. }} * {{eat | name=Karljohan | alt= | url=http://www.ravintolakarljohan.fi/?lang=en | email= | address=Yrjönkatu 21 | lat= 60.16799 | long=24.93921 | directions= | phone=+358 9 612-1121 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Very nice restaurant opposite the Hotel Torni with Swedish and Finnish traditional cooking and keeping high standards. On Thursdays, try the traditional lunch of pea soup and pancake. }} * {{eat | name=Lappi Restaurant | alt= | url=https://lappires.com/en/homepage/ | email= | address=Annankatu 22 | lat=60.16652 | long=24.93716 | directions= | phone=+358 9 645-550 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Although [[Finnish Lapland|Lapland]] (Lappi) is on the other edge of Finland, you can enjoy cuisine of Lapland in Helsinki too. }}
==Drink== ===Cafes=== [[File:Mutterikahvila.jpg|thumbnail|Is it a carousel? No, it's Café Mutteri!]]
* {{drink | name=Ateljee Baari | alt= | url= | email= | address= Kalevankatu 5 | lat= 60.16776 | long=24.93891 | directions=Hotel Torni (''14th floor'') | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Despite the name it's more like a cafe, located on top of Hotel Torni, Finland's first high-rise. Excellent views over Helsinki's downtown. You even have a view from the (famous) toilets. Highly recommended. Find the elevator close to the lobby to get there, but be prepared for expensive drinks. If you're on a tight budget, you can just enjoy the view on the elevator level. }} *{{drink | name=Banu Café | alt= | url=https://banu.fi/ | email=contact@banu.fi | address=Länsisatamankatu 24 A | lat=60.15719 | long=24.91210 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 40-373-3390 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A Persian fusion coffee shop with cozy bakery. }} *{{drink | name=Café Ekberg | alt= | url=http://www.ekberg.fi/en/ | email=info@ekberg.fi | address=Bulevardi 9 | lat=60.16482 | long=24.93782 | directions=in Kamppi | phone=+358 9 6811-860 | tollfree= | hours=M–F 07:30–19:00, Weekend 09:00–18:00 | price= | wikidata=Q11857235 | lastedit=2024-09-11 | content=One of the classic Helsinki cafés and a Bulevardi institution, which was founded in 1852. Was renovated in 2017 and at that lost some of its old flair. Ekberg has two premises right next to each other, with separate entraces. One of them is the main café which also functions as a lunch restaurant, the other is a bakery shop. }} *{{drink | name=Cafferino Oba | alt= | url=https://cafferinooba.com | email= | address=Kalevankatu 47 | lat=60.16438 | long=24.92928 | directions= | phone=+358 45-184-2038 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Café with a Mediterranean food. }} * {{drink | name=The Good Coffee Company | alt= | url= | email= | address=Eerikinkatu 33 | lat=60.164737 | long=24.927834 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Tu-F 10:00-17:00 | price= | lastedit=2023-08-16 | content=A quaint Austrian-style café in Kamppi. Serves Austrian coffee, Austrian beer and Austrian delicacies. }} * {{drink | name=Gran Delicato | alt= | url= | email= | address=Kalevankatu 34 | lat= 60.16524 | long=24.93242 | directions= | phone=+358 9 694-0403 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 08:00-22:00, Sa 10:00-18:00, Su closed | price=Coffees €2-4, salads €7-9, pastas and soups €7-9 | content=A very cosy cafe serving Mediterranean salads and paninis, and a variety of coffees. The Greek owner is a showman, usually around to amuse customers. }} * {{drink | name=Momochi | alt= | url=https://www.momochi.fi/ | email=info@momochi.fi | address=Mannerheimintie 20 | lat=60.1693 | long=24.9374 | directions=at Forum shopping centre (1st floor; Simonkatu entrance) | phone=+358 50-554-3300 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M–F 11:00–20:00, Sa 11:00–19:00, Su 12:00–18:00 | price= | content=A Japanese café. }} * {{drink | name=Cafe Mutteri | alt= | url=https://www.facebook.com/kahvilamutteri/ | email= | address=Lauttasaarentie 2 | lat=60.161377 | long=24.893039 | directions=Lauttasaari island | phone=+358 45-633-5418 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 06:00-19:00, Sa Su 10:00-16:00 | price= | content=Lovely small café in an octagonal old wooden building just at the Lauttasaari bridge. Its name translates to café "nut" as something you use with a bolt. Coffee, pies, pastries and ice cream are on offer. You can enjoy a slightly nostalgic atmosphere of the place, or just get pleasantly cosy on a freezing day. }}
===Bars, pubs and clubs=== [[File:Tavastia.jpg|thumbnail|Tavastia, where already the youth in the 1960s went to rock concerts]]
* {{drink | name=Angleterre | alt= | url= | email= | address=Fredrikinkatu 47 |lat=60.16631 | long=24.93448 | directions=at the corner of Kalevankatu and Fredrikinkatu | phone=+358 10-766-3580 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M Tu 15:00-00:00; W Th 15:00-01:00; F Sa 14:00-02:00; Su closed | price= | content=Oldest British pub in Helsinki since 1976. Only fifth outside the UK to gain the Cask Marque -award for excellence in serving real ale. }} * {{drink | name=Amsterdam | alt= | url= | email= | address=Itämerenkatu 1C |lat=60.16258 | long=24.91430 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Dutch beer house in Ruoholahti. }} * {{drink | name=A21 Cocktail Lounge | alt= | url=http://www.a21.fi/ | email= | address=Annankatu 21 | lat= 60.16669 | long=24.93741 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Drinks €7-25, try the ''Birch Cooler'', ''Blinker'' or ''XXX''. | content=A high-priced but incredibly high quality cocktail bar situated in the city center, a few hundred meters from Stockmann's. Generally serves classic cocktail drinks with a twist, but also features several cocktails with a distinctly Finnish vibe (featuring garnishes such as birch leaves and fresh cloudberry) and innovative house specialities. According to worldsbestbars.com, this unlikely find is one of the world's best bars [http://www.worldsbestbars.com/top-100.htm], winning the title of the best bar in the world in 2009 and 2010. }} * {{drink | name=Ravintola Wanhakettu | alt= | url=http://www.wanhakettu.fi/ | email= | address=Malminkatu 22 | lat= 60.167585 | long=24.929555 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Drinks €3.50-15, try the ''Karhu III Pint'', ''Salmari'' or ''Kolme Leijonaa Malt Whisky''. | content=A journey into traditional Finnish bar culture, where fireside debates and poetry recitals are a common place occurrence in this historical tavern. Frequented by a more intellectual if not boisterous crowd, the common traveler will find a home away from home at Wanhakettu. A famous haunt for many members of the classical artist communes of Käpyla and Suvela. According to historian Antti Mäjänen of the Ostrobotnious Historical Society, it was in this very spot that the second treaty of Vyborg was signed in the summer of 1902, adding character and flavor to this lovely tavern of old. }} * {{drink | name=Aussie Bar | alt= | url= | email= | address=Salomonkatu 5 | lat=60.17006 | long=24.93333 | directions=next to Kamppi shopping mall | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M Tu Su 14:00 - 02:00, W-Sa 12:00 - 03:00 | price= | content=The name says it. Place to go get some drinks with some international fun. Live music every Wednesday and the weekends are always crazy in Kangaroo Land. }} *{{drink | name=Corona Bar & Billiards | alt= | url=http://www.andorra.fi/en/corona/ | email= | address=Eerikinkatu 11 | lat=60.16694 | long=24.93413 | directions= | phone=+358 9 751-75611 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A bar and billiard hall owned by the film director brothers Aki and Mika Kaurismäki, echoing the melancholic mood of their films. Also check out the affiliated '''[https://andorra.fi/kafe-moskova/ Kafe Moskva]''' bar next door for authentic Soviet style experience, complete with Russian music played on dusty vinyls and Russian vodka and champagne. Downstairs is '''[http://www.andorra.fi/en/dubrovnik/ Dubrovnik]''', a small club-cum-movie theater that can be rented for private events and host occasionally live gigs or clubs. }} * {{drink | name=Crystal Show Club | alt= | url=https://www.crystalshowclub.com/#gref | email=info@crystalshowclub.com | address=Lönnrotinkatu 19 A | lat= 60.1651 | long=24.9347 | directions= | phone=+358 9 753-1731 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A strip club. }} * {{drink | name=Loose | alt= | url=http://www.barloose.com/ | email= | address=Annankatu 21 | lat=60.16671 | long=24.93739 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A very street-credible rock bar, it is highly popular among Finnish rock musicians. }} * {{drink | name=Le Bonk | alt= | url=http://lebonk.fi/ | email= | address=Yrjönkatu 24 | lat=60.16759 | long= 24.93914 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-Sa 21:00-04:00, Sa 16:00-04:00 | price= | content=Design music club with 3 floors. During the summer very nice roof terrace. }} * {{drink | name=Mann's Street | alt= | url= | email= | address=Mannerheimintie 12 | lat=60.16799 | long=24.94073 | directions=at Mercantile | phone=+358 9 612-1103 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=A gay karaoke bar. }} * {{drink | name=Old Irish Pub | alt= | url=https://oldirishpub.fi/en/page/etusivu | email=00100@oldirishpub.fi | address=Mannerheimintie 12 | lat=60.16812 | long=24.94056 | directions= | phone=+358 44-493-6655 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content= }} * {{drink | name=The Circus | alt= | url=http://www.thecircus.fi/ | email= | address=Salomonkatu 1-3 | lat= 60.17054 | long=24.93472 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Music venue club. }} <!--* {{drink | name=DTM | alt= | url=http://www.dtm.fi/ | email= | address=Currently closed & moving to new venue | lat=60.16709 | long=24.94199 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Daily 21:00-04:00 | price= | content=Formerly "Don't Tell Mama", DTM is the largest combination of gay cafe, bar, disco and nightclub in Scandinavia, with 2 dance floors and 3 bars. Popular among younger, trendy people and some celebrities. Straights usually welcome, too, as long as they don't "take over" the place. Entrance Su-Th €2.50, F €5, Sa €8 (on special nights fees may vary). }}--> *{{drink | name=Saaren Baari | alt= | url=https://saarenbaari.fi/ | email= | address=Tyynenmerenkatu 7 | lat=60.15746 | long=24.92145 | directions=in Jätkäsaari | phone=+358 400-617-442 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= }} * {{drink | name=Street Pride Bar | alt= | url=https://www.streetpride.fi/ | email=streetpridebar69@gmail.com | address=Malminrinne 1 | lat=60.16658 | long=24.93088 | directions=in Kamppi district | phone=+358 41-313-2010 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=A gay bar. }} * {{drink | name=Tavastia/Semifinal | alt= | url=http://www.tavastiaklubi.fi/index.php?lang=en | email= | address=Urho Kekkosen katu 4-6 | lat=60.16844 | long=24.93301 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=One of the most prominent rock clubs in Scandinavia, a must see for fans of live rock of any kind. Semifinal has smaller indie/alternative bands for a young crowd. On special nights the two clubs are joined, but usually they host separate gigs. Tickets for all gigs can be bought in advance from the Tiketti ticket sellers next door. The annual Tavastia new year party is an institution in itself, with fans flying from all over the world for the show.<br /><br />The space at Tavastia consists of two distinct halls with a door between them. One of them is the main music venue consisting of the main stage and a large space for the audience on two floors, the other has the cloakroom and the bar. The bar has a pretty basic selection of both alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks but no food. Note that while the bar has comfortable seats and tables, the main music venue is pretty much standing room only. }} * {{drink | name=The Tiger | alt=| url=http://www.thetiger.fi/ | email= | address=Urho Kekkosen katu 1 | lat=60.16908 | long= 24.93378 | directions=Kamppi Centre| phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Open W-Sa 22:00-04:00 | price=Cover charge €4-8, free entrance before midnight. | content=Formerly ''Lux'', this is a contestant for the title of Helsinki's classiest mainstream nightclub, with its dress code, stylish decor, relatively high prices and an age limit of 24. Five bars, two spacious terraces, a VIP lounge and six luxurious booths that can be reserved. Live music on Thur. }} * {{drink | name=El Patron | alt=former Jenny Woo, Vatican | url=https://www.elpatron.fi/ | email= | address=Simonkatu 6 | lat=60.16927 | long=24.93730 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Tries to profile itself as a nightclub for trendy young adults. You can lie on couches next to the crowded dance floor while sipping some bubbly. }}
==Sleep== [[File:Kellosaarenranta by night.JPG|thumbnail|Kellosaarenranta in Ruoholahti by night]]
===Budget=== * {{listing | name=City Center Seaside Apartment | alt= | url=https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/49999338 | email= | address=Azorienkuja 1 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2021-08-14 | content=Fully furnished apartment with a seaview in a new building next to the sport park and Tallink ferry terminal. }} * {{sleep | name=Omena Hotel |alt= | url= | lat= | long= | email= | address= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=4-person room from €45, "business" add-on €16; parking €17.50/night; "Omena" breakfast €5.60/person | checkin= | checkout= | lastedit=2020-02-24 | content=A self-service budget hotel concept with no front desk. Book and pay on the Internet and let yourself in with a passcode. Toilet, shower, 26" LCD-television, small fridge, microwave, water kettle + disposable cups, coffee, tea, hair dryer and dining table. {{mapmask|60.1666, 24.9436|60.1599, 24.9248|60.1555, 24.9249|60.138, 24.904|60.142, 24.840|60.172, 24.846|60.178, 24.868|60.169, 24.898|60.1650, 24.9209|60.1679, 24.9239|60.1722, 24.9352}}

{{Pagebanner|Helsinki skyline banner.jpg|dotm=yes|caption=Helsinki skyline|unesco_cc=yes}} {{printDistricts}}
'''Helsinki''' ([[Swedish phrasebook|Swedish]]: ''Helsingfors'') is [[Finland]]'s capital and largest city. Helsinki combines modern and historic architectural styles with beautiful open spaces. The city is surrounded by hundreds of tiny islands, and is a cultural bridge between the East and West. The "Pearl of the [[Baltic Sea]]" is easy to explore on foot or on bike, and it has a laid-back vibe.
==Districts== The city of Helsinki forms the core of Finland's largest urban area, known in Finnish as the "capital area" (''pääkaupunkiseutu''). Helsinki is bordered by the '''Gulf of Finland''' to the south, while the posh suburban city of '''[[Espoo]]''', with the embedded tiny enclave city of '''Kauniainen''', is to the west. The more industrialized city of '''[[Vantaa]]''' (where the airport is) is to the north and east. The Capital Region has a population of about 1.2 million, 650,000 of them living in Helsinki proper. Beyond these, the suburbs rapidly give way to small towns, farms and forests, most notably '''[[Nuuksio National Park]]''' at the intersection of Espoo, [[Vihti]] and [[Kirkkonummi]]. On the eastern side, at the tri-point of Helsinki, Vantaa and [[Sipoo]] you can find the '''[[Sipoonkorpi National Park]]'''.
Helsinki's city centre is on the southern peninsula at the end of the city's main thoroughfare '''[[Mannerheimintie]]''' (or just ''Mansku''). The central railway station and the main bus terminal are in the city centre. Shopping streets '''Aleksanterinkatu''' (or ''Aleksi'' for short) and '''Esplanadi''' (or ''Espa'') connect to '''Senate Square''' (''Senaatintori''), the historical centre of the city. See the [https://kartta.helsinginseutu.fi/index_en.html Helsinki Guide Map] for an interactive searchable map of the city.
{{Regionlist | regionmap=Helsinki Wikivoyage district map 2022.png | regionmaptext=Helsinki districts | regionmapsize=550px
| region1name=[[Helsinki/Central|Central]] | region1color=#d5dc76 | region1items= | region1description=The very core of Helsinki is, as one might expect, a lively part of the city with many attractions including the great art museums of Ateneum and Kiasma and the impressive central railway station. The district has many places to eat, drink and shop – streets are lined with these kinds of establishments, particularly the pedestrianised Aleksanterinkatu. For some relaxing greenery, two of the city's most notable parks are here, Esplanadinpuisto and Kaisaniemenpuisto.
| region2name=[[Helsinki/Kruununhaka and Katajanokka|Kruununhaka and Katajanokka]] | region2color=#71b37b | region2items= | region2description=The Lutheran Cathedral at the Senate Square with the surrounding buildings, dating from the early 19th century when Helsinki was made capital of the Grand Duchy of Finland, can be found here. One block south is the iconic waterfront with the Market Square, continuing east into Katajanokka. This can be considered the most "touristy" part of Helsinki, with many of the city's most famous buildings almost next to each other. The rest of Kruununhaka and Katajanokka are mainly residential.
| region3name=[[Helsinki/South|South]] | region3color=#69999f | region3items= | region3description=In the calm and affluent southern part of Helsinki you can enjoy the greenery of the parks and drop into a nice café for a cuppa coffee. Southern Helsinki also has its share of interesting museums, and if you're into fashion and design shopping, head to the district of Punavuori and along Fredrikinkatu. Suomenlinna, the fortress on an island, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and there are also some other islands just south of the mainland accessible to visitors.
| region4name=[[Helsinki/Kamppi and Southwest|Kamppi and Southwest]] | region4color=#8a84a3 | region4items= | region4description=For some great places for eating and drinking, head across Mannerheimintie and continue through Kamppi. Further west the former industrial part of the city, watched over by the cranes of the shipyard and industry chimneys you can nowadays glance over the modern architecture and out to the sea.
| region5name=[[Helsinki/West|West]] | region5color=#4f93c0 | region5items= | region5description=The western part of the city is a great getaway from the hustle and bustle of the city centre. If you have the time, take a leisurely [[A seaside stroll in Helsinki|seaside stroll]] along the shores of Laajalahti bay, or if you're a sports buff, visit the great summer and winter sports venues which are concentrated in this part of the city. The list cultural and historical sights of western Helsinki isn't bad either - it hosts the National Opera, Hietalahti cemetery, the Church in the Rock, the museums of Natural History, Finland's National Museum and the home of the long-time president Urho Kekkonen as well as [[Functionalist architecture in Finland|functionalist architecture]].
| region6name=[[Helsinki/Inner East|Inner East]] | region6color=#d56d76 | region6items= | region6description=If you, on the other hand, are interested in the more bohemian part of Helsinki and/or love to party you'd better head to the Inner East and districts like Kallio. The former working class part of the city is still associated with counterculture and to some extent left-wing politics and is largely inhabited by students. Kallio is as close as one could come to a "red light district" in Helsinki. However, the Inner East part of the city also hosts the amusement park [[Linnanmäki]] and the old wooden neighbourhoods of Vallila and Käpylä. The area also includes the increasingly populous district of Kalasatama, known for Finland's first skyscrapers.
| region7name=[[Helsinki/Eastern suburbs|Eastern suburbs]] | region7color=#d09440 | region7items= | region7description=The eastern parts of Helsinki is mostly residential and probably the most culturally diverse part of the city, as immigrants from many parts of the world live here. In this part of Helsinki you can find the Helsinki Zoo, the huge shopping complex Itis in Itäkeskus, as well as the northernmost metro station in the world in Mellunmäki. The [[Sipoonkorpi National Park]] spreads into the very easternmost parts of Helsinki.
| region8name=[[Helsinki/Northern suburbs|Northern suburbs]] | region8color=#ac5c91 | region8items= | region8description=The northern parts of Helsinki, which was once known as a separate rural municipality of Helsinki, now consists of highways, shopping malls, industries and residential buildings, plus quite a bit of greenery in-between. It connects seamlessly to the next city north of Helsinki: [[Vantaa]]. While not as culturally interesting as the other parts of Helsinki, it offers some natural attractions like the Central Park and Helsinki's highest point Malminkartanonhuippu.
}}
==Understand== [[File:Catedral Luterana de Helsinki, Finlandia, 2012-08-14, DD 14.JPG|thumb|300px|Helsinki's symbol, the Lutheran Cathedral (''Tuomiokirkko'')]]
Helsinki was established as a trading town by the [[Swedish Empire]] in 1550, but it wasn't until 1812, when the [[Russian Empire]] made it the capital of Finland, that it started growing. Today, this hustling and bustling city has 690,000 inhabitants and the capital area 1.36 million (2025). It is the northernmost capital of an EU member state. Helsinki metro is the northernmost subway in the world.
===History=== {{seealso|Nordic history}}
Helsinki was founded in AD 1550 by King Gustav Vasa of [[Swedish Empire|Sweden]], of which Finland was the eastern part, as a trading post to compete with [[Tallinn]] to the south in Estonia, which was Danish at that time. Helsinki was established about 5 km north-east of the current centre in the area today known as [[Vanhakaupunki]] ("the Old Town"), at the rapids where Vantaa river (at that time known as Helsinge river) flows into the Baltic Sea, which in turn gave the city its Swedish name ''Helsingfors'' ("Helsinge rapids"). Never becoming the trading post Gustav Vasa envisioned and largely bypassed by the [[King's Road (Finland)|King's Road]] — the major road along the coast — the village faced some hard times during its first centuries with fires and diseases; today there is but a few stones left of the original Helsinki. Eventually the city was moved further south to [[Helsinki/Central|its current location]], and in the middle of the 18th century, the maritime fortress Sveaborg (nowadays [[Suomenlinna]] in Finnish) was established on a handful of islands just in front of Helsinki, the fortress and the city together being nearly as populous as the regional capital [[Turku]]. The now world heritage-listed fortress archipelago features some of the oldest standing buildings in the city.
[[File:Helsinki 1820.jpg|thumb|250px|City Hall Square in 1820, before the rebuilding of [[Helsinki/Central|Central Helsinki]]]]
In 1809, through the Finnish War (a part of the [[Napoleonic Wars]]), Finland was annexed by [[Russian Empire|Russia]] and the capital of Finland moved from Turku to Helsinki in 1812, away from Sweden. The Czar felt the Grand Duchy of Finland needed a capital of grand proportions, and this was a major turning point in the history of Helsinki. The architects Johan Albrecht Ehrenström, a native Finn, and Carl Ludwig Engel, from Germany, were given the task of rebuilding the city in the Empire style. This can be seen today around the Lutheran Cathedral, which was completed in 1852. The same style, and even architects, is also a part of [[Saint Petersburg]]'s history.
Growing steadily during the 19th century, with the population exceeding 100,000 by the turn of the century, Helsinki established itself as the largest city and the political and cultural capital of the country. The city was a battleground in the Finnish Civil War in 1918, and was bombed by the Soviets in the [[World War II in Europe|Second World War]]. Until the 1950s Finland was largely an agricultural country, but the industrialisation and urbanisation that happened during the following decades meant an influx of new inhabitants from the rural parts of the country and Helsinki started becoming the metropolis it is today, with suburbs, superhighways and even a small subway system. Architecturally a young city, many of the monumental buildings erected since the independence have a stark modernist style and the rest of the city's architecture is made up of National Romantic style buildings from the early 20th century and the aforementioned Empire style buildings in Central Helsinki. Though thoroughly a Nordic capital, Helsinki today reflects the influences gained from the Western and Eastern cultures.
===Tourist information=== * {{listing | name=City of Helsinki Tourist Information Office | url=https://www.myhelsinki.fi/en/info/tourist-information | email=helsinki.touristinfo@hel.fi | address=Aleksanterinkatu 24 | lat=60.16895 | long=24.95226 | directions=southern side of the Senate Square | phone=+358 9 3101-3300 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=May–Sep: M–F 09:00–20:00, Sa Su 09:00–18:00; Oct–Apr: closes 18:00/16:00 | price= | content=A fount of information with helpful, multilingual staff. They also sell tickets to museums and sightseeing tours. }} There is also another one right in the Central Railway Station.
===Climate=== {{climate chart |Helsinki |-5.6 |-0.7 |53.0 |-6.3 |-1.3 |38.0 |-3.6 |2.3 |34.0 |1.1 |8.1 |34.0 |6.4 |14.6 |38.0 |11.2 |18.8 |60.0 |14.5 |21.9 |57.0 |13.5 |20.5 |81.0 |9.3 |15.4 |56.0 |4.2 |9.2 |73.0 |0.4 |4.4 |69.0 |-2.9 |1.4 |58.0
|float=right |clear=right |source=[[:w:Helsinki]] |description= }}
Helsinki is among the world's northernmost capitals and the lengthy '''winter''' is dark and chilly. Winter temperatures average {{Celsius|-5}}, but the wind chill and humidity makes it feel even colder and the mercury can plunge below {{Celsius|-20}} on a particularly cold day. Days are short. In general snow falls only intermittently and often melts into grey slush. However lake-effect snowfalls dropping copious amounts of snow during a few hours (and messing up the traffic) is not unheard of in the early winter. Since the [[Helsinki/Central|Helsinki peninsula]] juts into the sea, there is often a cold sea wind, and the climate is more maritime than [[Northern Helsinki|inland]], with snow and {{Celsius|-5}} replaced by slush, sleet and {{Celsius|0}}. This is especially apparent in November and December, but the first months of the year almost always have colder temperatures and clear skies.
The '''spring''' brings clear skies but the temperature doesn't increase as fast as the sunshine hours; even in April you may experience sub-zero nights, even as the afternoon temperatures raise above {{Celsius|10}}. The '''summer''' is often pleasant. Daytime temperatures average around {{C|21}} and drop to around {{c|13}} at night. There are some July and August afternoons that see temperatures around or above {{c|26}}, with warmer nights around {{c|16}}, and unlike the rest of the year you don't need a jacket or sweater even in the night. The sun sets late in the night and even then dusk turns into dawn without any real darkness in between. Parks burst into green, sunbathers dot the city's beaches and restaurants and bars deploy their terraces and patios, making the streetscape look more Central European for a couple of months. The '''autumn''' slowly develops throughout September with autumn foliage and moderate temperatures; snow and seriously cold weather before early November is very uncommon.
===Talk=== The city is officially bilingual, with an 86% [[Finnish phrasebook|Finnish]]-speaking majority and a visible 6% [[Swedish phrasebook|Swedish]]-speaking minority. Many in the Finnish-speaking majority only know the basics of Swedish, which they learned in school, while some speak it fluently.
Most people, especially in the younger generations, speak very good English. Although locals will appreciate an effort to say a few words in Finnish, they know very well how difficult Finnish is and will readily switch to English – many people also like the chance to practise their language skills.
Street signs and most other signs are usually in both Finnish and Swedish. In tourist-oriented areas, English signage is also prevalent. The Finnish and Swedish names of streets and places may differ significantly, for example Suomenlinna/Sveaborg for the fortress and Pasila/Böle for one of the train stations.
Being Finland's most cosmopolitan area by a fair margin, Helsinki also houses people from different parts of the world. While strolling around, you may hear people converse in Estonian, Russian, German, and Somali.
Authorities are required to give service in Finnish and Swedish, while many will speak English with foreigners and you may have luck with other languages. In touristy places, some staff will speak four or five languages.
==Get in==
===By plane=== {{main|Helsinki Airport}} [[File:HEL.January.2008.jpg|thumbnail|right|For most of the day, the Helsinki Airport is a cosy place resembling a large living room with a view, due to its wood-panelled flooring.]]
All international and domestic flights land at the compact, modern and airy {{go | name=Helsinki Airport | alt={{IATA|HEL}} | url=http://www.helsinkiairport.fi | email= | address= | lat=60.317222 | long=24.963333 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Helsinki Airport | image=Helsinki-vantaa aerial.jpg | wikidata=Q215327 | content= }}, which is in [[Vantaa]], 20 km to the north of central Helsinki.
The airport is well served from across Europe, from East Asia and was also well served from neighbouring Russia before Russia attacked Ukraine in 2022. There are also flights from some airports elsewhere in Asia and a few airports in the USA. Finnair used to use Helsinki's position to the north-east of Europe to offer competitive travel times to East Asia. However, since the Russian invasion of Ukraine, the Russian airspace cannot be used, which has nullified the advantage.
Coming from across the Baltic Sea, you may also consider flying to some regional airport with budget flights, such as [[Turku]] or [[Tallinn]], if their connections happen to suit. You can take a ferry from Tallinn or an intercity bus or train from the other cities, which generally have excellent connections to Helsinki (connecting flights are sparse, and you'd miss the city visit).
Helsinki Airport is on a commuter train loop, with trains every 10–30 minutes from 04:30 until 01:15, taking about 30 min to the centre. There are buses to the centre, to Vantaa, to Itäkeskus (with metro connection) in the eastern suburbs, and to other cities. Taxis are available. See [[Helsinki Airport#Ground transportation]] for details.
There are 24-hour grocery stores both at the airport (Alepa) and at Elielinaukio (S-Market), close to the Central Railway Station.
===By train=== [[File:Helsinki railwaystation 01.jpg|thumb|Central Railway Station]]
Being the national capital, Helsinki has direct services from all major train stations in Finland. The services from Russia stopped with the war on Ukraine. All long-distance trains to the city terminate at the {{go | name=Central Railway Station | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=60.171944 | long=24.941389 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Helsinki Central railway station | image=Helsinki Railway Station 20050604.jpg | wikidata=Q1044613 | content= }} which provides easy interchange to metro, bus, tram and local train lines. The train station offers bars, currency exchange, kiosks, fast food restaurants, luggage boxes and many other amenities, in addition to a central location. All trains also stop at {{go | name=Pasila station | alt=Böle station | url= | email= | address= | lat=60.1986 | long=24.9336 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Pasila station | image=Pasila railway station.jpg | wikidata=Q801316 | lastedit=2019-10-18 | content= }}, some 4 km north, which might be a good option if you're going elsewhere than central Helsinki. This is also where cars are loaded on trains to Lapland.
===By car=== Motorways connect Helsinki to [[Turku]] to the west, [[Tampere]] and [[Lahti]] [[Highway 4 (Finland)|to the north]], and to [[Porvoo]] and to [[Saint Petersburg]] in the east. The south and west of Finland are mostly surrounded by water, but you can bring your car on ferries from Tallinn (south, many daily connections), Stockholm (west, daily), or even Germany (south-west). See "By boat" below. There are ferries from Sweden also to [[Turku]] and [[Vaasa]] on the west coast of Finland.
===By bus=== Long-distance national and international coaches terminate at the underground {{Marker|type=go|lat=60.1690 | long=24.9321|name=Kamppi Bus Station}} (''Kampin linja-autoasema'') in the basement of the [http://www.kamppi.fi/en/ Kamppi mall] (''Kampin Keskus''). The station is adjacent to Mannerheimintie, directly connected to the Kamppi metro station and within a short walking distance from the Central Railway Station. The main connections are served both by traditional coaches and low-cost Onnibus double-deckers. <!-- The border to Russia is closed, so the info below does not apply: For travel from [[St. Petersburg]] (Russia), there are ordinary buses and minibuses. Ordinary buses are operated by [https://luxexpress.eu Lux Express], [https://ecolines.net Ecolines] and [http://bus.sovavto.com Sovavto]. Russian minibuses depart from the Oktyabrskaya Hotel (opposite the Moskovsky train station) around 22:00 and arrive behind Tennispalatsi at Eteläinen Rautatiekatu 8, one block away from Kamppi, early in the morning. Departures back start around 10:00 in the morning. The minibus trip costs around €15, making this by far the cheapest option, but the buses are cramped and uncomfortable and some of them stop at numerous supermarkets on the way so that Russian passengers can go for tax-free shopping. One of the operators is [https://www.transgold.ru/marshrutka-spb-helsinki/ Transgold]. -->
===By boat=== {{see also|Baltic Sea ferries|Boating in Finland}}
[[File:Silja Symphony and icy sea lane South Harbor Helsinki Finland.jpg|thumb|M/S Silja Symphony of the Stockholm line in Eteläsatama; the major ferries are made to go through even thick sea ice.]]
Helsinki is well connected with ferry services from [[Tallinn]], [[Estonia]], and [[Stockholm]], [[Sweden]], and there are limited services from [[Travemünde]] and [[Rostock]], [[Germany]]. Services from [[Saint Petersburg|St. Petersburg (Russia)]] haven't sailed since the start of Russia's war on Ukraine. There are occasional cruises from other ports.
For those continuing directly from Helsinki, many coaches to elsewhere start from the port and then continue via Kamppi, possibly with a transfer.<!-- no need to have this in every article with a connection via Helsinki -->
Ferries arrive at four harbours around the city: * {{go | name=West Harbour, Terminal 2 | alt=Länsisatama, Terminaali 2 | url=http://www.portofhelsinki.fi/en/cargo-traffic-and-ships/west-harbour| email= | address=Tyynenmerenkatu 14 | lat=60.1498 | long=24.9146 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2025-04-11 | content=Opened in 2017 on reclaimed land south of the older Terminal 1 that was demolished in 2025. [http://www.tallink.com Tallink's] and [http://www.eckeroline.fi Eckerö Line's] ferries from Tallinn dock here. Easy access by trams 7 and 9. }} * {{go | name=South Harbour, Olympia Terminal | alt=Eteläsatama, Olympiaterminaali | url=http://www.portofhelsinki.fi/en/cargo-traffic-and-ships/south-harbour | email= | address=Olympiaranta 1 | lat=60.1608 | long=24.9589 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2025-04-11 | content=West shore of the bay. [http://www.tallink.com Tallink Silja's] overnight cruise ferries from Stockholm, M/S Silja Serenade and M/S Silja Symphony, dock at Olympia Terminal. The terminal has an ATM, luggage lockers, a cafe-kiosk, and the Silja Line service point. The terminal was built for the 1952 [[Olympic Games]], hence the name. Trams 2 and 3 stop right outside the terminal. }}<!-- Makasiiniterminaali fell into disuse after Linda Line's bankruptcy in early 2018, and is closed as of April 2019. Might or might not reopen later. * {{go | name=South Harbour, Makasiini Terminal | alt=Eteläsatama, Makasiiniterminaali | url=http://www.portofhelsinki.fi/en/cargo-traffic-and-ships/south-harbour | email= | address=Eteläranta 7 | lat=60.1635 | long=24.9552| directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2017-10-24 | content=West shore of the bay. [http://en.lindaline.ee/ Linda Line] fast catamarans M/S Merilin and M/S Karolin arrive to Makasiini Terminal during open water season. The terminal has a kiosk, currency exchange, luggage lockers and Linda Line and Silja Line service points. Trams 2 and 3 pass near the terminal. }} --> * {{go | name=South Harbour, Katajanokka Terminal | alt=Katajanokan terminaali | url=http://www.portofhelsinki.fi/en/cargo-traffic-and-ships/south-harbour | email= | address=Katajanokanlaituri 8 | lat=60.1639 | long=24.9682 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2025-04-11 | content=East shore of the bay. [http://www.vikingline.fi Viking Line] ships (M/S Gabriella, M/S Cinderella, M/S Viking XPRS) arrive at Katajanokka Terminal. The terminal has a restaurant, kiosk, an ATM, and the Viking Line service point. The terminus of tram 5 (only operates around departures and arrivals of ferries) is in front of the terminal, while tram 4 stops a few blocks towards the centre. }} * {{go | name=Vuosaari Harbour, Hansa Terminal | alt=Vuosaaren satama, Hansaterminaali | url=http://www.portofhelsinki.fi/en/cargo-traffic-and-ships/Vuosaari-harbour | email= | address=Provianttikatu 5 | lat=60.2216 | long=25.1760 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2025-04-11 | content=Mainly a cargo port, but used also by [http://www.finnlines.com Finnlines] services from [[Travemünde]]. Take bus 90 to Vuosaari and continue by metro. }}
See the [https://www.portofhelsinki.fi/en/ Port of Helsinki site] for the latest details.
==== By ferry from Tallinn ==== The [http://www.ts.ee ferry route] from [[Tallinn]], [[Estonia]] to Helsinki has upwards of 20 departures daily. Depending on the ferry, journey time is anywhere from 1½ to 3½ hours. Prices average €16–30 one way, depending on operator, season (summer costs more), day of week (Fridays and Saturdays cost more) and time of day (out in the morning and back in the evening is popular and hence more expensive). The route is among the most popular international ferry routes in the world (7.3 million passengers in 2025). Particularly popular are '''day cruises''', which can go for as little as €15 return. Booze is significantly cheaper in Estonia than in Finland so a significant share of passengers will take advantage of the price difference by getting drunk in Tallinn or already on the way there. All ferries also carry cars, from €25 one way. Bicycles can be taken for around €5 one way. Bikes on car ferries must go through vehicle check-in, which closes earlier than passenger check-in, so plan for extra time.
The following companies operate ferries between Tallinn and Helsinki: * {{listing | name=Eckerö Line | alt= | url=http://www.eckeroline.fi/en/default.aspx | email=info.ee@eckeroline.ee | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+372 664 6000 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= |wikidata=|lastedit=2019-02-19 | content=Operates only one ship, the 2000-passenger ''Finlandia'' (three times daily, travel time 2½ hr). Often has cheap fares. }} * {{listing | name=Tallink Silja | alt= | url=http://www.tallinksilja.com | email=booking@tallink.ee | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+372 640 9808 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2015-03-24 | content=Up to 6 departures daily on large Shuttles Star and Superstar (2 hr). Discounts are available to Eurail pass holders. As this company has more departures you will have more flexibility planning your day trip. }} * {{listing | name=Viking Line | alt= | url=http://www.vikingline.fi | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= |wikidata=|lastedit=2020-02-24| content=Large ''Viking XPRS'' ferry (2½ hr, 2 sailings a day). Usually cheapest. }}
Eckerö and Viking usually have the cheaper fares, as they are more geared towards day-trippers and the party crowd who come to have a great time on board and tend to spend more in the bars, restaurants and shops on board.
====Arriving by yacht==== Beware of the very busy ferry traffic, especially the fast ferries from Tallinn. Use boating routes if possible.
